text_original: The people do not fear death;
text_normalized: The people do not fear death;

text_original: to what purpose is it to (try to) frighten them with death?
text_normalized: to what purpose is it to (try to) frighten them with death?

text_original: If the people were always in awe of death, and I could always seize those who do wrong, and put them to death, who would dare to do wrong?
text_normalized: If the people were always in awe of death, and I could always seize those who do wrong, and put them to death, who would dare to do wrong?

text_original: There is always One who presides over the infliction death.
text_normalized: There is always One who presides over the infliction death.

text_original: He who would inflict death in the room of him who so presides over it may be described as hewing wood instead of a great carpenter.
text_normalized: He who would inflict death in the room of him who so presides over it may be described as hewing wood instead of a great carpenter.

text_original: Seldom is it that he who undertakes the hewing, instead of the great carpenter, does not cut his own hands!
text_normalized: Seldom is it that he who undertakes the hewing, instead of the great carpenter, does not cut his own hands!
